name: Q2080069
abstract: Samuel David Moore (October 12, 1935 – January 10, 2025) was an American singer who was best known as a member of the soul and R&B duo Sam & Dave from 1961 to 1981. He was a member of the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, the Grammy Hall of Fame (for "Soul Man"), the Vocal Group Hall of Fame, and the National Rhythm & Blues Hall of Fame.

about_quotes.speaker: Lew Soloff
about_quotes.evidence: Elton John, Billy Joel, Sting, Gladys Knight, Sam Moore who lives here, from Sam & Dave, who lives in Phoenix, Tom Jones, Martha from Martha and the Vandellas. Stevie Wonder sat in. And I’ll tell you it was just thrilling. It was thrilling to play this music with these people.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lew Soloff interviewed by Monk Rowe, Scottsdale, Arizona, April 16, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: George Lewis
about_quotes.evidence: I forget his first name. I never knew his first name. ... Landry couldn't do it. Sam Moore couldn't do it. Sam Moore couldn't do it. William was the leader.

about_quotes.speaker: Albert Jiles
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] ...he plays with the Tuxedo now. That one Alfred used to be with Sam Moore in years and years. That's right. [INTERVIEWEE] That's right.

about_quotes.speaker: John Casimir
about_quotes.evidence: Sam Moore and Chris Kelly had a crowd of all the kids right there. And they went in and they put a package on.
